food_name: Starbucks Pike Place Roast Ground Coffee

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301339 covers roasted coffee bean as the closest parent; no specific entry exists for Starbucks Pike Place Roast branded ground coffee.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers roasted coffee as the closest parent category; no branded product-level code exists for Pike Place Roast.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34658001 covers coffee as a substance; closest parent used as no specific code exists for this branded roast.

serving.description: 1 tablespoon ground coffee (for one 180 ml cup)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving is approximately 7 g (1 rounded tablespoon) of ground coffee used to brew one 180 ml cup; the 250 g retail bag yields approximately 35 servings.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, brewed from grounds, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 14209); dry ground coffee values extrapolated from USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods entry for roasted ground coffee
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are reported per 100 g of dry roasted ground coffee, sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for roasted ground coffee (NDB 14201 / FDC ID 171890). Pike Place Roast is a medium-roast Arabica blend; no branded USDA entry exists for this specific product. Values are adapted from the closest USDA SR Legacy entry for roasted ground coffee. The product is sold as dry ground coffee, not as a brewed beverage; per-100g values reflect the dry powder form.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of coffee and coffee-based beverages — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature consensus
gi_source.source_match_notes: Dry roasted ground coffee contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature consistently classify plain coffee as having an effectively zero glycemic response. Per the zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) to satisfy numeric validation requirements. gi_category is low. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because the dry coffee powder contributes no glycemic carbohydrates when brewed and consumed as a plain beverage.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium; caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Plain brewed Pike Place Roast contains negligible sodium (approximately 2 mg per 240 ml cup) and poses minimal hypertension risk; however, individuals with caffeine sensitivity should limit intake to 1–2 cups per day as caffeine can transiently raise systolic blood pressure by 3–5 mmHg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible glycemic carbohydrates; chlorogenic acids may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain brewed Pike Place Roast has a negligible glycemic load and does not raise postprandial blood glucose; epidemiological evidence suggests moderate unsweetened coffee consumption (3–4 cups/day) is associated with reduced type 2 diabetes risk, likely via chlorogenic acid-mediated improvements in insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat; antioxidant polyphenols; moderate caffeine
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pike Place Roast brewed coffee is very low in saturated fat and contains antioxidant chlorogenic acids and diterpenes; moderate consumption (3–5 cups/day of filtered coffee) is associated with neutral to mildly protective cardiovascular outcomes, though unfiltered brewing methods may raise LDL cholesterol due to cafestol and kahweol.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Starbucks Pike Place Roast is a medium-roast Arabica ground coffee sold in a 250 g retail bag, named after the original Starbucks store at Pike Place Market in Seattle, Washington. The roast profile is well-rounded and smooth, with tasting notes of cocoa and toasted nuts, characteristic of a balanced medium roast that avoids the sharpness of light roasts and the bitterness of dark roasts. The coffee is made from a blend of Latin American Arabica beans roasted to a medium level, producing a rich amber brew with moderate acidity and body. It is available as pre-ground coffee or as whole beans with custom grind options available in Starbucks stores. Nutritionally, dry roasted ground coffee is energy-dense per 100 g (approximately 331 kcal) due to its concentrated dry-powder form, but a standard 7 g serving brewed into 180–240 ml of water contributes negligible calories, carbohydrates, fat, and protein to the diet. The brewed beverage is essentially calorie-free when consumed without milk or sugar. Coffee is a significant dietary source of niacin (vitamin B3) and potassium. It contains chlorogenic acids, a class of polyphenol antioxidants associated with anti-inflammatory effects and potential improvements in insulin sensitivity. Caffeine content is approximately 95–120 mg per 240 ml brewed cup. The product is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following gluten-free or dairy-free diets. It is sold at Starbucks outlets in Singapore, including the One Fullerton location, at SGD 17.70 for a 250 g bag.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Starbucks Pike Place Roast?
Is Pike Place Roast suitable for diabetics?
What does Pike Place Roast taste like?
Is Starbucks Pike Place Roast medium or dark roast?

Is Pike Place Roast gluten free?

confidence_reason: No USDA Branded Food entry exists specifically for Starbucks Pike Place Roast 250g. Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for roasted ground coffee (FDC ID 171890), which is the closest approved-source match. The product description and serving context are confirmed from the field survey data input record and Starbucks product information. GI is estimated by convention for plain coffee. Confidence is medium rather than high because the nutrient values are adapted rather than exact-matched to this specific branded product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of dry roasted ground coffee powder, not per 100 ml of brewed beverage. A standard 7 g serving brewed into 240 ml contributes approximately 2 kcal, 0.3 g protein, 0.0 g fat, and 0.0 g carbohydrate to the diet. 2. sugar_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for roasted ground coffee; dry roasted coffee contains no free sugars. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for roasted coffee. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; coffee beans contain no cholesterol. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; roasting destroys ascorbic acid in coffee beans. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for roasted coffee. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero; plant-derived food contains no B12. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for roasted ground coffee. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero; plant-derived food contains no vitamin D. 10. vitamin_e_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for roasted ground coffee. 11. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this product contains no glycemic carbohydrates when brewed; gi_category is low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. 12. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.1) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1) are approximate values from USDA SR Legacy; exact values for this specific blend may vary slightly. 13. The product is sold as a 250 g retail bag at SGD 17.70 at Starbucks Singapore (One Fullerton). 14. Multilingual local_names are null as this is a US-origin branded commercial product with no established local-language names in Singapore or other regional markets. 15. G6 multilingual compliance: this food is classified under Coffee cuisine with US origin; it is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish, and therefore no mandatory local-language names are required under the G6 multilingual naming rules.

improvement_actions_taken: Set glycemic_index to 1 (not null or 0) per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule.
Set gi_category to low per convention for plain coffee.
Documented all zero nutrient values with confirmed zero or naturally zero justification phrases in notes.
Used only exact, closest_parent, or none for all ontology match_type fields.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent.
Confirmed G6 multilingual compliance: Coffee/US-origin product does not require Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese mandatory local-language names.
Documented all -1 sentinel values are not present (replaced with actual USDA values where available, or 0.0 with justification where confirmed zero).
Set nutrient values per 100g of dry ground coffee powder, not brewed beverage, with clear documentation.
